evals.prompt: Design a system architecture for a real-time ride-sharing platform. It needs to handle 100K concurrent drivers, match riders to drivers within 3 seconds, process 1M trips per day, and maintain 99.9% uptime. Include the data model, key services, and technology choices.
evals.expected_output: A comprehensive system architecture with C4 models, technology choices, trade-off analysis, and data model
expectations: Output covers the high-level system components (matching service, location service, payment, etc.)
Output addresses the real-time matching requirement with specific technology approach
Output discusses scaling strategy for 100K concurrent drivers
Output includes data model for trips, drivers, and riders

evals.prompt: Review this architecture decision: our team wants to move from a monolith to microservices for our 3-year-old e-commerce platform. We have 5 engineers, 50K daily active users, and the main complaint is deployment speed — releases take 4 hours and happen monthly. Is microservices the right answer?
evals.expected_output: An architecture review with honest trade-off analysis, alternative options, and a recommendation
expectations: Output challenges whether microservices is the right solution for the stated problem
Output identifies that slow deployments may be a CI/CD problem, not an architecture problem
Output presents alternative approaches (modular monolith, strangler fig, CI/CD improvement)
Output analyzes the cost of microservices for a 5-engineer team
Output provides a clear recommendation with rationale
